===== Graph helper scripts for Aixa =====
/home/azureuser/aixa-graph-token.sh
/home/azureuser/aixa-graph-token.sh

===== aixa-graph-token.sh contents (if it exists) =====
#!/bin/bash
# aixa-graph-token.sh — fetch a delegated Graph token on Aixa's behalf.
# One-time bootstrap: Aixa completes a device-code consent flow against the
# Microsoft Graph PowerShell public client. The resulting refresh_token is
# stashed in SDN-YooVault as aixa-graph-refresh-token.
set -euo pipefail
RT=$(az keyvault secret show --vault-name SDN-YooVault --name aixa-graph-refresh-token --query value -o tsv)
RESP=$(curl -s -X POST 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/oauth2/v2.0/token' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_id=82ac9df1-618f-4c16-ac69-c9983fa313fe' \
  --data-urlencode 'grant_type=refresh_token' \
  --data-urlencode "refresh_token=$RT" \
  --data-urlencode 'scope=Mail.ReadWrite Mail.Send Calendars.ReadWrite Contacts.ReadWrite Chat.ReadWrite Files.ReadWrite.All Notes.ReadWrite.All User.Read Sites.ReadWrite.All TeamsAppInstallation.ReadWriteForChat offline_access')
NEW_RT=$(echo "$RESP" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get("refresh_token",""))')
if [ -n "$NEW_RT" ]; then
  echo -n "$NEW_RT" | az keyvault secret set --vault-name SDN-YooVault --name aixa-graph-refresh-token --file /dev/stdin -o none
fi
echo "$RESP" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)["access_token"])'
